name: Thai Squid Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Squid; clean, cut into 1-inch squares
recipeInstructions:
2 tablespoons Fish sauce
2 tablespoons Lime juice
1 to 2 teaspoons Thai chile paste (or chili paste or hot pepper sauce)
2 teaspoons Sugar
2 tablespoons Canola oil
2 Cloves garlic, minced
2 Thai or jalapeno chiles, seeded and sliced thinly
1 cup Fresh mint leaves
1. Lightly score the Squid squares in a crosshatch pattern using a sharp knife; do not cut all the way through.
2. Cut off the top 2/3 of each lemon grass stalk and remove roots and outer leaves. Thinly slice the core on a diagonal. (If using dried lemon grass, soak in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain.)
3. Place lettuce leaves on a platter or salad plates and arrange tomato wedges on top.
4. Prepare the dressing by whisking together fish sauce, lime juice, chile paste, and sugar in a small bowl until smooth.
5. Just before serving, heat oil in a wok over high flame. Swirl in the oil, add garlic, chiles, and lemon grass, and stir-fry for 20 seconds or until fragrant.
6. Add Squid and onions to the wok and stir-fry until the Squid is firm and opaque, about 1 minute.
7. Stir in half of the mint leaves. Spoon the squid mixture over the tomatoes and lettuce leaves.
8. Top with remaining mint leaves and serve immediately.
